items.scenario: You recently joined the campus fitness center on a semester membership, but the climbing wall you signed up to use has been closed for repairs for two weeks. The facilities manager oversees all equipment servicing.
items.direction: Write an email to the facilities manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain how the closure affects your membership
Ask when the climbing wall will reopen
Suggest offering an extension on the membership
items.subject: Climbing wall closure at the fitness center

items.scenario: You are a first-year student whose research paper is due Friday, but you were ill for several days and fell behind on the reading. Your instructor sometimes grants short extensions when a student explains the situation early.
items.direction: Write an email to your instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the illness that set you back
Ask whether a short extension is possible
Mention how much extra time you would need
items.subject: Extension for Friday's research paper

items.scenario: You bought tickets to a jazz festival organized by the campus music society, but the confirmation lists two seats instead of the four you paid for. The organizer manages all ticket orders for the event.
items.direction: Write an email to the festival organizer. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the error in your ticket order
Ask how the missing seats can be added
Tell the organizer how many tickets you paid for
items.subject: Wrong number of festival tickets

items.scenario: You and a friend planned a weekend hiking trip next month, but the forecast now predicts heavy rain for Saturday. You would rather adjust the plans than cancel the trip that you both looked forward to.
items.direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the weather problem for Saturday
Suggest shifting the hike to Sunday
Ask which day works better for your friend
items.subject: Rain in the forecast for our hike
